content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听，并回答第21至第24题。
content.en_text: Now, listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 24.

content.cn_text: Andy：嗨，Andy。研究进展怎么样？
content.en_text: Andy: Hi, Annie. How's the research going?

content.cn_text: Annie：还可以吧，但我有点担心我们的展示主题。
content.en_text: Annie: OK, I guess, but I'm concerned about our presentation topic.

content.cn_text: 我们主要要讨论产品是如何针对男孩和女孩进行不同营销的，
content.en_text: We're mainly going to be talking about how products are marketed differently for boys and girls,

content.cn_text: 我们知道这在一些国家确实如此，但它在所有地方都是这样吗？
content.en_text: and we know that's true in some countries, but is it the case everywhere?

content.cn_text: Andy：我一直在读英国的情况。
content.en_text: Andy: I've been reading about the situation in the UK.

content.cn_text: 一些大型零售商一直在给产品贴标签，说明某个产品是面向哪种性别的。
content.en_text: Some of the major retailers have been labeling their products to say which gender a product is meant for.

content.cn_text: Annie：真的吗？
content.en_text: Annie: Really?

content.cn_text: Andy：是的。显然，与科学科目相关的玩具被标为男孩用，
content.en_text: Andy: Yes. Apparently, toys connected to science subjects are labeled for boys,

content.cn_text: 而像厨房设备这样的东西则标为女孩用。
content.en_text: and stuff like kitchen equipment says for girls.

content.cn_text: Annie：其他国家也会这样吗？
content.en_text: Annie: Does that happen in other countries too?

content.cn_text: 希望我们的调查能告诉我们，这是不是一种全球现象。
content.en_text: Hopefully our survey can tell us that, whether it's a global phenomenon or not.

content.cn_text: Andy：嗯。
content.en_text: Andy: Hmm.

content.cn_text: 不过这很有意思，因为在英国，很多家长都投诉这种标签方式，
content.en_text: But it's interesting, because in the UK, so many parents have complained about this kind of labeling

content.cn_text: 以至于大型零售商不得不放弃它。
content.en_text: that the major retailers are having to abandon it.

content.cn_text: 由于社交媒体，消费者可以给零售商施加更大压力，迫使他们取消不受欢迎的政策。
content.en_text: Because of social media, consumers can put a lot more pressure on retailers to drop unpopular policies.

content.cn_text: Annie: 说到商店如何陈列玩具，大多数家长会觉得有用的是，
content.en_text: Annie: When it comes to how stores display toys, what most parents would find useful

content.cn_text: 商店标明孩子应该多大，
content.en_text: is for stores to indicate how old the child should be,

content.cn_text: 这样玩具就不会过于复杂。
content.en_text: so the toy isn't overly complicated.

content.cn_text: 我确信家长并不需要按性别贴标签。
content.en_text: I'm sure parents don't need gender-specific labeling.

content.cn_text: 你自己就能看出大致主题是什么，
content.en_text: You can see for yourself what the general idea is,

content.cn_text: 无论是化学类还是搭建类。
content.en_text: whether it's for chemistry or building.

content.cn_text: Andy：那位神经科学家 Lise Eliot 写的那本书。
content.en_text: Andy: That book by that neuroscientist, Lise Eliot.

content.cn_text: 嗯，叫什么来着？
content.en_text: Well, what was it called?
Annie: Pink Brain, Blue Brain. Annie：《Pink Brain, Blue Brain》。

content.cn_text: Andy：对，就是它。
content.en_text: Andy: That's it.

content.cn_text: 里面有很多关于广告如何影响儿童的内容。
content.en_text: There was a lot of material on the way advertising can affect children.

content.cn_text: 她的论点确实很有道理。
content.en_text: Her arguments really make sense.

content.cn_text: Annie：是的，有些观点非常有说服力。
content.en_text: Annie: Yes, some claims are very convincing.

content.cn_text: 尽管她主要谈的是美国，但我确信这些观点也可以适用于其他地方。
content.en_text: Even though she's mainly talking about the USA, I'm sure they can be applied elsewhere.

content.cn_text: Andy：我标出的一点是，按性别营销产品如何
content.en_text: Andy: One thing I highlighted was how marketing products, according to gender,

content.cn_text: 会给孩子未来带来问题。
content.en_text: can cause problems for children in the future.

content.cn_text: 例如，如果你向女孩传递科学和工程类东西不适合她们的信息，
content.en_text: For example, if you signal to girls that science and engineering stuff isn't for them,

content.cn_text: 那么她们长大后肯定会认为那是男孩做的事情。
content.en_text: then surely they grow up believing that that's what boys do.

content.cn_text: 即使她们在学校学习科学和物理，
content.en_text: Even if they study science and physics at school,

content.cn_text: 她们也可能不会把这些科目看作未来谋生的方向。
content.en_text: they might not regard those subjects as something they'll eventually make a living from.

content.cn_text: Annie: 所以那些职业就会成为她们不会考虑的工作，
content.en_text: Annie: So those are jobs they won't consider,

content.cn_text: 而且如果你从不鼓励男孩玩培养社交能力的玩具，
content.en_text: and if you never encourage boys to play with toys that develop socialization skills,

content.cn_text: 这也不公平。
content.en_text: that's not fair either.

content.cn_text: Andy：也许吧，但后果没那么严重。
content.en_text: Andy: Maybe, but the consequences aren't as problematic.

content.cn_text: Annie：嗯，我不太确定。
content.en_text: Annie: Hmm, not sure about that.

content.cn_text: 其实，我认为这是我们展示中应该讨论的内容。
content.en_text: Actually, I think that's something we should address during the presentation.

content.cn_text: 在听讨论的剩余部分之前，
content.en_text: Before you hear the rest of the discussion,

content.cn_text: 你有一些时间查看第25至第30题。
content.en_text: you have some time to look at questions 25 to 30.

content.cn_text: 现在请听，并回答第25至第30题。
content.en_text: Now listen and answer questions 25 to 30.

content.cn_text: Annie：我们来梳理一下儿童服装这一部分吧。
content.en_text: Annie: Let's talk through the section about clothes for children.

content.cn_text: Andy: 也就是我们如何形成了每种性别对应特定颜色的情况。
content.en_text: Andy: The way we've ended up with specific colours for each gender.

content.cn_text: Annie：我觉得令人惊讶的一点是，19世纪美国的孩子过去穿白色衣服。
content.en_text: Annie：One thing I found surprising is that children in 19th century America used to be dressed in white.

content.cn_text: Andy: 是的，一直到6岁。
content.en_text: Andy: Yes, until age 6.

content.cn_text: 这是因为这些衣服更容易清洗。
content.en_text: It was because the outfits were a lot easier to clean.

content.cn_text: Annie：是的，他们只要漂白就能去掉污渍。
content.en_text: Annie: Yes, they just bleached them to get the dirt out.

content.cn_text: 这和时尚或成本无关。
content.en_text: Nothing to do with fashion or cost.

content.cn_text: 当时没有其他有效的清洗方法。
content.en_text: There was no other effective way to clean them.

content.cn_text: Andy：然后在20世纪初，我读到男孩穿粉色，女孩穿蓝色。
content.en_text: Andy: Then, in the early 1900s, I read that boys were dressed in pink and girls in blue.

content.cn_text: 显然，粉色被描述为强势，而蓝色被视为柔和。
content.en_text: Apparently pink was described as dominant, whereas blue was seen as delicate.

content.cn_text: Annie：是的，而且普通人，也就是购物者，
content.en_text: Annie: Yes, and ordinary people shoppers, I mean.

content.cn_text: 他们会读到关于名人子女的文章，
content.en_text: They could read articles about the sons and daughters of the famous

content.cn_text: 了解他们如何穿衣，然后希望自己的孩子也穿成那样。
content.en_text: and how they were dressed and they wanted their own children to be dressed the same.

content.cn_text: Andy：但到了20世纪40年代，颜色已经改变了，而且更多与顾客需求有关。
content.en_text: Andy: But by the 1940s, the colours had changed and it was more about customer demand.

content.cn_text: 零售商分析了家长的购物习惯，并查看结果。
content.en_text: Retailers analysed the shopping habits of parents and looked at the results.

content.cn_text: 他们将其解读为，粉色现在是女孩最喜欢的颜色，蓝色是男孩最喜欢的颜色。
content.en_text: And they interpreted them to mean that pink was now the favourite colour for girls and blue for boys.

content.cn_text: 于是商店开始相应地宣传这些产品。
content.en_text: So, shops started to advertise the products accordingly.

content.cn_text: Annie：是的，在美国，那时男孩像爸爸一样穿，女孩像妈妈一样穿。
content.en_text: Annie: Yes, and in the USA, it was a time when boys dressed like dad and girls like mum.

content.cn_text: 直到20世纪60年代，少女们才拒绝这种做法。
content.en_text: It wasn't until the 1960s that teenage girls rejected that.

content.cn_text: 所有少女开始穿男孩或女孩都可以穿的衣服。
content.en_text: And all the teenage girls started wearing clothes that could have been worn by either boys or girls.

content.cn_text: 通过这样做，她们觉得通常只向男性开放的工作机会现在也会向她们开放。
content.en_text: By doing that, they felt the job opportunities usually only open to men would now be available to them.

content.cn_text: Andy：但到了20世纪80年代，电视广告第一次专门针对儿童，
content.en_text: Andy: But then in the 1980s, for the first time, TV commercials targeted children specifically,

content.cn_text: 推广男孩和女孩不同类型的衣服。
content.en_text: promoting different types of clothes for boys and girls.

content.cn_text: 结果，孩子们开始坚持让父母给他们买“正确类型”的衣服。
content.en_text: And as a result, children started insisting their parents buy them the right kind of clothes.

content.cn_text: Annie: 这种消费行为将很难改变。
content.en_text: Annie: That kind of consumer behaviour will be hard to change.

content.cn_text: 还有一位研究者 Paoletti 认为，那些小时候从未穿过漂亮的典型女性化服装的女性，
content.en_text: And one researcher, Paoletti, she maintains that women who never got to wear pretty typically feminine clothes when they were little,

content.cn_text: 现在会让自己的女儿穿那种风格的衣服，以弥补这一点。
content.en_text: were then now dressing their girls in that style to make up for it.

content.cn_text: 她们希望自己的女儿拥有她们当年没有的东西。
content.en_text: They want their girls to have what they didn't.

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

groups.desc: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
groups.title: Questions 21–26

items.title: According to John, UK retailers who differentiate between boys’ and girls’ products are

options.title: A
options.content: being forced to change this approach.

options.title: B
options.content: only responding to what customers want.

options.title: C
options.content: responsible for establishing a global trend.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第21题问英国区分男孩和女孩产品的零售商现在怎样。原文说 retailers are having to abandon it，说明他们被迫改变这种做法，所以答案是 A. being forced to change this approach。

items.title: Annie believes most parents prefer children’s toys to be displayed according to
items.analysis: 答案解析：第22题问 Annie认为大多数家长更希望儿童玩具按什么陈列。原文说 indicate how old the child should be，所以答案是 C. age group。

items.title: When discussing Lise Eliot’s book on gender roles, the students agree that

options.title: A
options.content: Eliot has overestimated the influence of advertising on children.

options.title: B
options.content: Eliot’s book will be most popular within the USA.

options.title: C
options.content: Eliot makes some valid points within the book.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第23题问学生们讨论 Lise Eliot 关于性别角色的书时达成什么共识。Andy 说 her arguments really make sense，Annie 也说 some claims are very convincing，所以答案是 C. Eliot makes some valid points within the book。

items.title: In John’s opinion, the problem of marketing toys according to gender is that a child’s
items.analysis: 答案解析：第24题问 Andy 认为按性别营销玩具的问题是什么。原文说 girls might not regard science and engineering as something they'll make a living from, so those are jobs they won't consider，说明职业选择可能受限，所以答案是 B. career options could become limited。

items.title: It was because of _____.  that children wore white clothes in the 19th century.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第25题问19世纪儿童穿白色衣服是因为什么。原文说 outfits were easier to clean，并且 no other effective way to clean them，属于实际原因，所以答案是 H. practical reasons。

items.title: In the early 1900s, consumers were influenced by the choices of _____. .
items.analysis: 答案解析：第26题问20世纪初消费者受到谁的选择影响。原文说 articles about the sons and daughters of the famous and how they were dressed，所以答案是 G. well-known figures。

items.title: By the 1940s, shop owners believed that _____.  were blue for boys and pink for girls.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第27题问到20世纪40年代，店主认为蓝色给男孩、粉色给女孩是因为什么。原文说 retailers analysed the shopping habits of parents，并按这些结果理解颜色偏好，所以答案是 A. parental preferences。

items.title: In the 1960s, teenage girls believed that _____.  could be beneficial to them.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第28题问20世纪60年代少女认为什么可能对她们有利。原文说 they started wearing clothes that could have been worn by either boys or girls，也就是 unisex styles，并认为这样能带来工作机会，所以答案是 F. unisex styles。

items.title: The _____.  of the 1980s meant that people felt pressure to shop for gender-specific clothing.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第29题问20世纪80年代的什么使人们感到购买性别化服装的压力。原文说 TV commercials targeted children specifically，promoting different types of clothes for boys and girls，属于广告变化，所以答案是 E. advertising changes。

items.title: Nowadays, some mothers want their daughters to have _____. , as they did not have these themselves.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第30题问如今一些母亲希望女儿拥有什么，因为她们自己小时候没有。原文说 women who never got to wear pretty typically feminine clothes now dress their girls in that style，所以答案是 D. feminine clothes。

source_credit: 此内容由🍠@Listening 永不虾滑整理，仅作为分享供个人使用；禁止商用、倒卖和引流。有问题请先仔细看🍠这个账号的置顶和分享贴。没有团队，不是机构，纯个人分享。督学和其他相关课程请咨询主页另一个号@心情有点虾滑（不回任何机经相关消息）。
